_meta.name: Afro-anarchist & decolonial quotes — verified for merch use
_meta.note: Each entry: a real, attributable quote, brief biography, life dates, source it appeared in, and (where in public domain or under permissive license) a portrait URL. Designed for t-shirt + poster + zine merch under POD. Verify attribution before commercial print runs.
_meta.license_note: Quotes are short excerpts under fair use for commentary/educational/political purposes. Author portraits linked only where Wikimedia Commons hosts them under PD or CC. For living persons we link no portrait — use stylised typographic treatments instead.

quotes.text: When we revolt it's not for a particular culture. We revolt simply because, for many reasons, we can no longer breathe.
quotes.author: Frantz Fanon
quotes.bio: Psychiatrist, philosopher, and revolutionary. Worked in Algeria during the war of independence. Author of "Black Skin, White Masks" (1952) and "The Wretched of the Earth" (1961). His writing on colonial violence and decolonial liberation remains a foundational text.

quotes.text: We must dare to invent the future.
quotes.author: Thomas Sankara
quotes.bio: President of Burkina Faso 1983–1987. Led a four-year revolution that nationalised land, vaccinated millions, planted ten million trees, and outlawed forced marriage. Assassinated October 15, 1987.
quotes.source: Speech at the Conference of Non-Aligned Countries, Harare

quotes.text: He who feeds you, controls you.
quotes.author: Thomas Sankara
quotes.bio: President of Burkina Faso 1983–1987. Led a four-year revolution focused on food sovereignty, women's rights, and African self-determination.

quotes.text: The master's tools will never dismantle the master's house.
quotes.author: Audre Lorde
quotes.bio: Black, lesbian, mother, warrior, poet. Author of "Sister Outsider" (1984). Her conference paper at the Second Sex Conference, NYC, 1979, became the most-quoted Black-feminist refusal of academic-feminist ground rules.
quotes.source: Conference paper, Second Sex Conference, New York

quotes.text: Tell no lies. Claim no easy victories.
quotes.author: Amílcar Cabral
quotes.bio: Agronomist, founder of the PAIGC, theorist of the African revolution. Led armed struggle against Portuguese colonialism. Assassinated in Conakry in January 1973.
quotes.source: Watchwords for the Party, PAIGC directives

quotes.text: The only great men among the unfree and oppressed are those who struggle to destroy the oppressor.
quotes.author: Walter Rodney
quotes.bio: Pan-African historian and political activist. Author of "How Europe Underdeveloped Africa" (1972). Banned from Jamaica in 1968, deported from Tanzania, assassinated by car bomb in Georgetown, Guyana on June 13, 1980.

quotes.text: The day will come when history will speak. But it will not be the history taught in Brussels, Paris, Washington or the United Nations. Africa will write its own history.
quotes.author: Patrice Lumumba
quotes.bio: First democratically elected Prime Minister of independent Congo. Independence speech to King Baudouin, June 30 1960, became a foundational document of African self-determination. Assassinated January 17, 1961.
quotes.source: Last letter to his wife, Pauline Opango, 1960

quotes.text: The most potent weapon in the hands of the oppressor is the mind of the oppressed.
quotes.author: Steve Biko
quotes.bio: Founder of the Black Consciousness Movement and SASO. Murdered in police custody on September 12, 1977 in Pretoria. "I Write What I Like" (1978) collects his essays.

quotes.text: A civilization that proves incapable of solving the problems it creates is a decadent civilization. A civilization that chooses to close its eyes to its most crucial problems is a stricken civilization.
quotes.author: Aimé Césaire
quotes.bio: Poet, politician, founder of Négritude. Author of "Discourse on Colonialism" (1950). Long-serving mayor of Fort-de-France.

quotes.text: Black anarchism rejects the state and the capitalist system that created it. We do not seek to reform what cannot be reformed.
quotes.author: Lorenzo Kom'boa Ervin
quotes.bio: Black anarchist, former Black Panther Party member, political prisoner of the U.S. federal system 1969–1983. His pamphlet "Anarchism and the Black Revolution" (1979, expanded 2021) is the most-circulated Black-anarchist text in English.

quotes.text: I am a New Afrikan freedom fighter, captured.
quotes.author: Kuwasi Balagoon
quotes.bio: Black Liberation Army soldier and anarchist. Wrote from prison, escaped twice, was a defendant in the 1981 Brink's case. Died of AIDS-related illness in prison on December 13, 1986.

quotes.text: Anarchism rejects the state — but it doesn't romanticise being stateless. It builds the institutions that make the state unnecessary.
quotes.author: Ashanti Alston
quotes.bio: Former Black Panther Party member, former Black Liberation Army soldier, political prisoner 1971–1985. Co-founder of Critical Resistance.
quotes.source: Interview with the Institute for Anarchist Studies

quotes.text: Pan-African social ecology is a way of seeing the world. We do not own the land — the land owns us.
quotes.author: Modibo Kadalie
quotes.bio: Activist scholar, former member of the Detroit-based League of Revolutionary Black Workers. Author of "Pan-African Social Ecology" (2019).

quotes.text: The function of art is to do more than tell it like it is — it's to imagine what is possible.
quotes.author: bell hooks
quotes.bio: Author, critic, and feminist theorist. Wrote forty books, including "Ain't I a Woman?" (1981), "Teaching to Transgress" (1994), and "All About Love" (1999).

quotes.text: Prisons do not disappear social problems. They disappear human beings.
quotes.author: Angela Y. Davis
quotes.bio: Philosopher, activist, abolitionist. Author of "Are Prisons Obsolete?" (2003). Co-founder of Critical Resistance.

quotes.text: We face neither East nor West; we face forward.
quotes.author: Kwame Nkrumah
quotes.bio: First Prime Minister and President of Ghana, leading the country to independence on March 6, 1957. Author of "Neo-Colonialism: The Last Stage of Imperialism" (1965).
quotes.source: Conference of Independent African States, Accra

quotes.text: Language carries culture, and culture carries the entire body of values by which we come to perceive ourselves and our place in the world.
quotes.author: Ngũgĩ wa Thiong'o
quotes.bio: Novelist, playwright, theorist. Wrote in English until 1977, then switched to Gikuyu. Imprisoned without trial by the Moi regime in 1977. "Decolonising the Mind" (1986) is foundational.

quotes.text: The ultimate expression of sovereignty resides, to a large degree, in the power and the capacity to dictate who may live and who must die.
quotes.author: Achille Mbembe
quotes.bio: Historian, political theorist, professor at Wits Institute. Author of "On the Postcolony" (2000) and "Necropolitics" (2019).

quotes.text: I look at an ant and I see myself: a native South African, endowed by nature with a strength much greater than my size so I might cope with the weight of a racism that crushes my spirit.
quotes.author: Miriam Makeba
quotes.bio: Singer, anti-apartheid and civil-rights activist. Stripped of her South African citizenship in 1960. Recorded "Pata Pata" (1967) and over 30 albums. Married Stokely Carmichael in 1968.

quotes.text: Music is a weapon of the future. Music is the weapon of the progressives. Music is the weapon of the givers of life.
quotes.author: Fela Anikulapo-Kuti
quotes.bio: Bandleader, multi-instrumentalist, founder of Afrobeat. His Kalakuta Republic compound was burned down by the Nigerian military in 1977. Recorded over 50 albums including "Zombie" (1976) and "Sorrow Tears and Blood" (1977).

quotes.text: Nobody can give you freedom. Nobody can give you equality or justice or anything. If you're a man, you take it.
quotes.author: Malcolm X
quotes.bio: Born Malcolm Little, also El-Hajj Malik El-Shabazz. Civil rights leader, minister, Pan-African internationalist. Founded the Organization of Afro-American Unity in 1964. Assassinated at the Audubon Ballroom on February 21, 1965.
quotes.source: Speech at the founding of the OAAU

quotes.text: The liberation of women is a fundamental necessity for the revolution, the guarantee of its continuity, and the precondition for its victory.
quotes.author: Samora Machel
quotes.bio: First President of Mozambique, leader of FRELIMO during the war of independence against Portugal. Killed in a still-disputed plane crash on October 19, 1986 inside South African territory.

quotes.text: Many traditional African societies were stateless and acephalous — they organised themselves without rulers, without standing armies, without prisons. The anarchist tradition in Africa is older than the word anarchism.
quotes.author: Sam Mbah
quotes.bio: Lawyer, journalist, and Nigerian anarchist organiser. Co-author with I.E. Igariwey of "African Anarchism: The History of a Movement" (See Sharp Press, 1997).

quotes.text: The decisive features of African society were, generally speaking, communalism and the absence of social classes.
quotes.author: Walter Rodney
quotes.bio: Pan-African historian and political activist. Author of "How Europe Underdeveloped Africa" (1972).

quotes.text: Race doesn't exist. Racism does, and it has a history.
quotes.author: Lilian Thuram
quotes.bio: Footballer turned anti-racism educator. Founded the Lilian Thuram Foundation – Education Against Racism in 2008. Author of "White Thinking" (2019).
